frame_analyses.description: Video opens with title card 'Part 1/2 Intracorporeal Lap Suturing' with tagline 'let's knot waste any time!'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content indicator: title and branding overlay present

frame_analyses.description: Overhead view of FLS trainer box with Penrose drain on pink suture block. Two needle drivers visible (black and silver handles). Drain has longitudinal slit marked with two green target markers.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard Task 5 setup confirmed. Pre-marked entry/exit points visible.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver (black handle) approaching needle on right side of field. Needle orientation appears to be for right-hand leading entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle grasp point appears to be at approximately mid-shaft, adequate for backhand technique

frame_analyses.description: Silver needle driver grasps suture at entry mark. Both drivers positioned for needle passage. Drain slit clearly visible between marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perpendicular entry angle observed. Entry point aligned with left green marker.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through drain. Black driver holds proximal suture. Silver driver advances needle through slit in controlled motion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth needle passage, no excessive manipulation visible

frame_analyses.description: Needle exits drain slit, approaching right-side mark. Both suture ends now visible extending from drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point appears aligned with right green marker. Clean single pass.

frame_analyses.description: Black driver wraps suture around silver driver shaft. Clear double-wrap formation (surgeon's knot first throw) visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right-hand (black) leading first throw. Two complete wraps observed before cinching.

frame_analyses.description: First throw being cinched down. Black driver pulls wrap tight against drain. Knot tightens at marked line location.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate tension applied. Knot sits on drain slit closure line.

frame_analyses.description: Hand switch observed. Silver driver now leads second throw, wrapping around black driver shaft once (single throw).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand alternation from throw 1 to throw 2. Left hand (silver) leading second throw.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw cinched. Silver driver pulls single wrap tight. Knot stack grows at drain interface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Single throw confirmed. Tension controlled, knot secure.

frame_analyses.description: Hand switch again: black driver (right) leads third throw, single wrap around silver driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand alternation from throw 2 to throw 3. Right hand (black) leading.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw cinched. Final knot tightened securely. All three throws complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete knot sequence observed: surgeon's knot (double) + single + single with proper hand switching.

frame_analyses.description: Knot now seated firmly at marked line on drain. Drain slit appears fully apposed beneath knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: No visible gap in drain slit. Knot appears tight and secure.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors (not visible in earlier frames) positioned to cut suture tails. One tail being cut by left-side driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting phase initiated. First suture end cut inside trainer box.

frame_analyses.description: Second suture tail being cut. Both ends cut near knot to minimize tail length.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cuts. Minimal suture remaining distal to knot.

frame_analyses.description: Task completion shown. Drain with completed knot at marked line, slit fully apposed. Both suture tails cut. All instruments withdrawn.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears completed successfully. Knot secure, drain intact, closure achieved.

completion_time_rationale: This is educational/instructional content with title cards, scene transitions, and narrative framing. Actual elapsed time during skill execution is obscured by editing, title screens, and tutorial formatting. No continuous timer or frame-by-frame counting is visible.

task_specific_assessments.video_type_note: This is instructional/demonstration content, not a scored performance. Scoring is not applicable.

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap, appropriate tension, knot seated at marked line
Single wrap observed, hand switched correctly from throw 1
Single wrap observed, hand switched correctly from throw 2. Final knot tight.

suture_placement.notes: Entry and exit points appear aligned with green markers, but exact millimeter deviation cannot be measured from edited instructional video.

drain_assessment.notes: Drain slit fully apposed under knot. No visible gap or separation.

score_components.formula_applied: Score cannot be computed for instructional content
score_components.reason_not_scored: This video is classified as instructional/educational demonstration content with narrative framing, title cards, and scene transitions. It is not a continuous live performance with measurable completion time. Scoring requires a continuous timed performance video.

confidence_rationale: This is instructional content, not a performance. Confidence in scoring is zero because scoring is not applicable. The video demonstrates correct technique clearly but is edited and narrated, not a raw performance suitable for FLS rubric scoring.
cannot_determine: Completion time — video is edited with title screens and transitions
Exact phase timings — editing obscures continuous timing
Precise suture deviation in millimeters — camera angles adequate but no measurement tool visible
Frame-by-frame analysis of actual elapsed time — not provided in edited format
video_classification_rationale: This video is labeled 'Part 1/2' with title overlays, has a narrator-style tagline ('let's knot waste any time!'), shows transitions between scenes, and is explicitly announced as exclusive to an app and members-only section. These are hallmarks of instructional/educational content rather than a raw performance recording. The final frame explicitly states 'Next Part 10 — Live demo of intra-corporeal lap suturing exclusively on App & Member's only Section on YT', confirming this is tutorial content.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle grasp at mid-shaft for backhand entry
Perpendicular needle entry angle to drain slit
Alternating hand dominance between throws (right → left → right)
Knot tensioning at marked line location
Drain slit full apposition under knot
Suture tail cutting inside trainer box with minimal tail length remaining
Smooth hand and instrument transitions between throws
verbal_cues: Tagline: 'let's knot waste any time!' (humorous reference to knot-tying task)
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding oblique needle entry (demonstrated perpendicular entry)
Ensuring proper hand alternation across all three throws (explicit hand switching shown)
Preventing gap in drain slit (knot seated directly at closure site with full apposition)
Avoiding loose knots (appropriate tension applied at each throw)
Proper first throw as double-wrap (surgeon's knot shown clearly)
teaching_content.teaching_observations: This is a high-quality instructional video demonstrating expert-level intracorporeal suturing technique. All procedural steps are executed cleanly with proper hand alternation, appropriate knot tensioning, and successful drain slit closure. The demonstration is designed for educational purposes and appears in a curated instructional series.

improvement_suggestions: Not applicable — this is instructional content demonstrating correct technique, not a trainee performance
summary: This video is an instructional/educational demonstration of FLS Task 5 (Intracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying). It is NOT a raw performance video and therefore cannot be scored using the FLS rubric. The content clearly demonstrates expert-level technique including proper needle placement, surgeon's knot formation, correct hand alternation across three throws, secure knot tensioning, complete drain slit closure, and proper suture cutting. The video is edited with title screens, transitions, and narrative framing, confirming its classification as instructional material rather than a continuous timed performance. For scoring purposes, a raw, unedited, continuous performance recording is required.
